source.title: AI Product Manager Technical Foundations
source.text: PRIVATE SOURCE TEXT: proprietary notes about model serving, evaluation, and product risk.

enrichment_items.title: Evaluation Clip
enrichment_items.text: PRIVATE VIDEO TRANSCRIPT: user-owned explanation of regression evals.

teaching_layers.content: Agent evaluation connects product promises to repeatable evidence. A product manager should define target behavior, failure modes, fixtures, and regression thresholds before trusting an AI workflow.

content: Regression eval: a repeated test that catches quality drift.
Fixture: a stable input/output case used for repeatable evaluation.
Human mastery gate: a checkpoint that asks the operator to understand the change before accepting risk.

quiz_items.prompt: Why should an AI product workflow define eval fixtures before launch?
quiz_items.rubric: Mention target behavior, failure modes, repeatability, and launch confidence.

answers.text: PRIVATE ANSWER TEXT: eval fixtures make product risk visible before release.

grading_results.feedback: PRIVATE FEEDBACK: the answer is strong but should mention regression thresholds.

insights: The learner is ready to compare native fast gates with optional external judge frameworks.
The next lesson should ask the learner to design one failed case and one passing case.
scribe_log: A safe public scribe note would focus on eval shape, not private answer text.
